finding: Site: medulla with variable degree of cortical involvement

differentials.name: sudden / gradual onset of lower chest / upper abdominal / flank / back pain; shocklike symptoms = signs of massive blood loss
differentials.raw: sudden / gradual onset of lower chest / upper abdominal / flank / back pain; shocklike symptoms = signs of massive blood loss

differentials.name: acute adrenal insufficiency (rare): manifesting as hypotension + hyponatremia + hyperkalemia
differentials.raw: acute adrenal insufficiency (rare): manifesting as hypotension + hyponatremia + hyperkalemia

differentials.name: Underlying adrenal mass: adrenocortical adenoma / carcinoma, myelolipoma, pheochromocytoma, metastasis, neuroblastoma

differentials.name: bilateral hemorrhage: acute primary adrenal insufficiency (rare) is life- threatening
differentials.raw: bilateral hemorrhage: acute primary adrenal insufficiency (rare) is life- threatening

context: √  round / oval mass displacing kidney inferiorly + IVC anteriorly √  uniform adrenal enlargement gradually decreasing in size over 6-8 weeks (follow-up for 2-3 months) √  complete resolution with time: √  ± rimlike curvilinear / eggshell calcifications > 1 year √  no enhancement NECT: @   acute stage (< 7 days): √  round / oval mass (in 83%) located in medulla + stretching cortex around hematoma: √  hyperattenuating (50-90 HU) similar to most neoplasms in acute stage → slowly decreasing over time N.B.:   Follow-up required to confirm regression! √  obliteration of gland by diffuse irregular h
